untranslatable_reason: This signal is an OPTIONS STRATEGY (calendar spread with call expiration mechanics) that cannot be expressed using price-based technical indicators alone. It requires: (1) tracking of specific option contract states (near-term call expiration worthlessness), (2) multi-leg option position management (sell near-term call, buy long-term call, then sell intermediate-term call), and (3) option Greeks and time decay dynamics. The backtest engine appears to be equity price-based only and lacks option contract state tracking, greeks (delta, theta, vega), and multi-leg position management capabilities.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_90 is a tactical options strategy from Lawrence McMillan's work. Entry requires manual detection of: calendar spread establishment + near-term call expiration worthless + execution of second calendar spread. Exit is author-silent (untranslatable). This requires an OPTIONS-ENABLED backtest engine with contract-level position tracking and Greeks calculations, not a standard price indicator engine.
